lines.en_lit: Rather, Rava said: He presents downward seven for the purpose of the bull, and he again presents upward once and downward seven for the purpose of the goat.
cups became mixed, He presents and he again presents
And he again presents three times.
If part of blood became mixed and part of the blood did not become mixed, obvious: When he presents, he presents from the certain
However, the other Are they remainder and goes to the base Or perhaps is rejected, and go to the canal
Rav Pappa said: Even according to the one who says one cup renders its counterpart a remainder, this applies where he could present if he wanted However, this as not be presented if he wanted not

Rav Huna, son of Rav Yehoshua, said to Rav Pappa: On the contrary, even according to the one who says one cup renders its counterpart rejected, that applies he rejects with hands, However, where he does not reject it with hands, not
As it was taught above, it says: and he shall pour its blood And below it says: and he shall pour all its blood

From where with regard to a sin-offering that one collected its blood in four cups and presented once from this and once from that that all is poured on the base The verse states: “And he shall pour all its blood.”

might even he presented four presentations from one of them, the verse states: “And he shall pour its blood.” that is poured on the base and the are poured into the canal. Rabbi Eliezer, son of Rabbi Shimon, says: From where with regard to a sin-offering that if one collected its blood in four cups and presented four presentations from one of them, that they are all poured at the base The verse states: “And he shall pour all its blood.”
And according to Rabbi Eliezer, son of Rabbi Shimon, it written: “And he shall pour its blood”? Rav Ashi said: to exclude remainders that are in the throat of animal.

he poured the blood of the bull into the blood of the goat. We learned in accordance with the one who said mixes on the corners As it was stated: Rabbi Yoshiya and Rabbi Yonatan, One said: mixes and one said: He does not mix
Conclude Rabbi Yoshiya was who said mixes although Together, is not written it is considered as though Together, is written.
Even you say Rabbi Yonatan, it is different here, as it is written “once”
It was taught contrary to our response, and he shall take of the blood of the bull and of the blood of the goat, that be mixed. the statement of Rabbi Yoshiya.
